action.content:
Is the following true? "People use time to buy money." Explain your answer in a way that makes it easy for kids to understand. Include the word "farmer". Exclude the words "economy", "demand" and "supply".

First REPEAT the ENTIRE REQUEST ABOVE, word for word without change, then give your answer. Please do not say any words or characters before repeating the request.

***

No, that sentence is not true. It is actually the other way around! People usually spend their money to buy more time.

Think about a **farmer**. If the farmer wants to rest and play with his family, he might hire someone else to help in the fields. He uses his money to pay that person, so he can save his own time.

Sometimes, people work hard to earn money so they can buy things that make their lives easier, like a robot vacuum or a fast car. These things help them save time. So, we trade our time to get money, and then we use that money to get back some of our time or to enjoy our free moments.
action.label: response
